rootCause: PublicMobileAppLayer was imported and rendered, but mobile route still displayed the legacy hero as the first viewport, so real iPhone users saw the unchanged old homepage first.
Public and admin mobile visibility rules used max-width: 640px rather than the requested safer max-width: 768px.
AdminMobileAppLayer was rendered after login, but the mobile topbar still preceded it, reducing first-viewport app-layer clarity.

publicMobileRouteBinding: Changed public mobile app layer media query to max-width: 768px.
Hid the legacy home hero on mobile index route so PublicMobileAppLayer starts at top: 0.
Removed negative app-shell offset and made public mobile layer min-height: 100svh.
Added polished Mobile App brand sentinel.
adminMobileRouteBinding: Changed admin mobile app layer media query to max-width: 768px.
Hid desktop admin topbar on mobile so AdminMobileAppLayer is the first post-login viewport.
Added polished Admin App brand sentinel.

noticeReflection.method: localStorage admin store reflection smoke on deployed origin

verification.consoleError: PASS 0 errors on deployed public/notice browser check

remainingHold: Actual iPhone Safari manual confirmation by the user is still external to this automated proof.
Server auth/D1/R2 production operation remains HOLD; this patch intentionally did not change it.
Admin after-login screenshots use a CDP sessionStorage auth seed because the in-app browser text input path was blocked by a clipboard extension issue.
